description: The retry loop stops after the final backoff without performing the corresponding last rename attempt.
grading_hint: Look for the strict loop bound combined with indexing backoffs[attempt]; the last sleep is now wasted and one retry is lost.

description: The fsync throttle default changed to 1500 ms while the public comment still promises a 1000 ms default.
grading_hint: Compare the fallback passed to positiveIntEnv with the documented default immediately above it.

description: Normal settlement marks the operation settled before cleanup, so the new guard prevents the progress interval from ever being cleared.
grading_hint: Trace settle(): settled becomes true before _clearProgressTimer() calls this guarded cleanup.

description: An unescaped cwd is interpolated into shell source after policy scanning, allowing shell metacharacters in the directory value to inject commands.
grading_hint: The policy checks only command, while cwd is later concatenated into commandWithCwd and passed to the shell.

description: The exit-code write is started without awaiting it before the done marker is written, violating the completion protocol ordering.
grading_hint: Consumers treat donePath as proof that exitPath is complete; the first write promise is not awaited.

description: A failed or corrupt session read silently becomes a blank live session, which callers can subsequently persist over the original data.
grading_hint: Inspect loadSession's catch fallback and follow its use by close/resume paths that save the returned object.

description: The cleanup gate is inverted, so cleanup is skipped when the runtime is idle and runs only while some runtime work is active.
grading_hint: Compare hasActiveRuntimeWork() with the cleanup function's intended idle-only execution.
